note: seq=94: SPEED UP the pipe -- shorten the autopull timer cadence 2min->45s so each clean apply is followed quickly by the next manifest. Guardrail-compliant (keeps Unit=atlas-autopull.service + OnUnitActiveSec + WantedBy=timers.target so it can NEVER sever the pipe; the puller's post-apply health check re-verifies autopull-timer-enabled+active). The puller ALREADY cache-busts its fetch (?cb=<epoch> + Cache-Control:no-cache), so this interval cut is the biggest remaining latency win available via the pipe. NOTE: changing the FETCH URL to the GitHub API/commit-pinned raw (to kill residual raw-CDN propagation lag) requires editing /opt/atlas/autopull/atlas-autopull.sh, which FORBID_PATH_RE blocks via manifest by design -- that needs an out-of-band (SSH) edit; flagged, not attempted here. Keeps one-at-a-time + integrity-gate + rollback.

steps.content:
[Unit]
Description=ATLAS auto-pull (fast 45s cadence)

[Timer]
OnBootSec=15s
OnUnitActiveSec=45s
Unit=atlas-autopull.service

[Install]
WantedBy=timers.target

steps.cmd: /opt/atlas/venv/bin/python -c "import subprocess as s; s.run(['systemctl','daemon-reload']); s.run(['systemctl','restart','atlas-autopull.timer']); print('autopull cadence -> 45s')"
